posts.author: Quicko
posts.text: Taxation for us stock trading — Hey @Shubham_Dudeja1 , if the gains are in the segment of F&O or intraday then it should be shown as business income but if gains are from equities then it should be reported under capital gains while filing ITR!

posts.author: Shubham_Dudeja1
posts.text: Taxation for us stock trading — As I file my ITR in business income. Should I not file business income for US stocks also, held for short term?

posts.author: Nsingh
posts.text: Taxation on Open Position — Thanks @Jason_Castelino

posts.author: Jason_Castelino
posts.text: Taxation on Open Position — Same.

posts.author: Nsingh
posts.text: Taxation on Open Position — Thanks @Quicko , What about your view @Jason_Castelino .

posts.author: Quicko
posts.text: Taxation on Open Position — Hey @Nsingh , If the broker balance is positive, you can show it under Other Current Assets in ITR-4. If the broker balance is negative, you can show it under Other Current Liabilities.

posts.author: Singrauli
posts.text: Taxation for us stock trading — Indian residents cannot trade US options/futures under RBI’s LRS rules. US stock investing is allowed, but derivatives are restricted.

posts.author: Nsingh
posts.text: Taxation on Open Position — Thanks @Jason_Castelino , And what about broker balance, where we have to show, debtors or other assets in ITR 4. Moreover, if there is unrealised gain in futures (and same is withdrawn), then broker balance turn into negative balance, then where we have to show, creditors or other liabilities in ITR 4.

posts.author: kvnreddy
posts.text: Taxation for us stock trading — Are indian residents allowed to do options trading in us markets, if so which broker provides that feature

posts.author: Quicko
posts.text: Taxation for us stock trading — Hello @Shubham_Dudeja1 It depends on type of gains or losses. For example, if gains are from trading in F&O in US market it is treated as a business income and taxable at slab rates. If its through equity deliveries, then STCG ( if held for less than 24 months) would also be taxable at slab rates and for LTCG the tax rate is 12.5%.

posts.author: Jason_Castelino
posts.text: Taxation on Open Position — Not required. It’s indirectly already reduced from your balance with broker.

posts.author: Nsingh
posts.text: Taxation on Open Position — @Jason_Castelino , waiting for your reply.

posts.author: BB789
posts.text: Taxation on Open Position — Not really. Only some details in balance sheet like cash in hand, sundry debtors are mandatory in itr4 and the rest are optional - the balance sheet doesn’t have to balance and the system will still accept.

Actually my question is f&o have two components, one is options and second one is futures, in options there is no mark to market settlement but in case of futures, there is mark to market settlement, and you know if you have unrealised loss at the year end, let’s say ₹ 10 lacs, this means ₹ 10 lacs had gone from our trading account to unknown country party account. I just want to know whether this amount shown in balance sheet, specially for those who file ‘itr 4 sugam’ in presumptive basis (but disclose complete details of ba…

posts.author: Sankalp
posts.text: Taxation for us stock trading — @Quicko

posts.author: Shubham_Dudeja1
posts.text: Taxation for us stock trading — Hello everyone, I am looking for resources to know what are tax implication for me if I start trading in US market through INDMONEY. I file my ITR as business income as most of my income comes from Equity Delivery, Intraday or FnO.

posts.author: Jason_Castelino
posts.text: Taxation on Open Position — No. If it was equity bought for trading purpose, it can be stock in trade. If it’s fno, they are mere contracts. Basically right to buy or sell. So it can be unrealised gains or loss under current assets or current liabilities. Anyways it won’t make any difference to tax liability and it hardly matters unless it is some 10cr plus turnover. If it’s above 10cr, then it’s anyways audit case. So keep it simple.

posts.author: BB789
posts.text: Taxation on Open Position — Shouldn’t it be stock in trade as F&O is not a capital asset?

posts.author: Jason_Castelino
posts.text: Taxation on Open Position — Current asset or current liability. As the case may be.

posts.author: Nsingh
posts.text: Taxation on Open Position — Hey @Jason_Castelino , where to show open position of f&o at the year end in balance sheet
